name: python-ternary

metadata.name: python-ternary
metadata.summary: Make ternary plots in python with matplotlib

documentation:
python-ternary is a plotting library for use with matplotlib to make ternary
plots, including heatmaps.

modules.name: ternary.helpers
documentation: Helper functions and utilities for projecting to the simplex and various tasks.

functions.name: ternary.helpers.unzip
documentation: ## Auxilliary Functions ###

functions.name: ternary.helpers.normalize
documentation:
Normalizes input list.

Parameters
----------
l: list
    The list to be normalized

Returns
-------
The normalized list or numpy array

Raises
------
ValueError, if the list sums to zero

functions.name: ternary.helpers.simplex_iterator
documentation:
Systematically iterates through a lattice of points on the 2-simplex.

Parameters
----------
scale: Int
    The normalized scale of the simplex, i.e. N such that points (x,y,z)
    satisify x + y + z == N

boundary: bool, True
    Include the boundary points (tuples where at least one
    coordinate is zero)

Yields
------
3-tuples, There are binom(n+2, 2) points (the triangular
number for scale + 1, less 3*(scale+1) if boundary=False

functions.name: ternary.helpers.permute_point
documentation:
Permutes the point according to the permutation keyword argument. The
default permutation is "012" which does not change the order of the
coordinate. To rotate counterclockwise, use "120" and to rotate clockwise
use "201".
# Ternary Projections ##

functions.name: ternary.helpers.project_point
documentation:
Maps (x,y,z) coordinates to planar simplex.

Parameters
----------
p: 3-tuple
    The point to be projected p = (x, y, z)
permutation: string, None, equivalent to "012"
    The order of the coordinates, counterclockwise from the origin

functions.name: ternary.helpers.planar_to_coordinates
documentation:
Planar simplex (regular x,y) to maps (x,y,z) ternary coordinates. The order of the coordinates is counterclockwise
from the origin.

Parameters
----------
p: 2-tuple
    The planar simplex (x, y) point to be transformed to maps (x,y,z) coordinates

scale: Int
    The normalized scale of the simplex, i.e. N such that points (x,y,z)
    satisfy x + y + z == N

functions.name: ternary.helpers.project_sequence
documentation:
Projects a point or sequence of points using `project_point` to lists xs, ys
for plotting with Matplotlib.

Parameters
----------
s, Sequence-like
    The sequence of points (3-tuples) to be projected.

Returns
-------
xs, ys: The sequence of projected points in coordinates as two lists

functions.name: ternary.helpers.convert_coordinates
documentation:
Convert a 3-tuple in data coordinates into to simplex data
coordinates for plotting.

Parameters
----------
q: 3-tuple
   the point to be plotted in data coordinates

conversion: dict
    keys = ['b','l','r']
    values = lambda function giving the conversion
axisorder: String giving the order of the axes for the coordinate tuple
    e.g. 'blr' for bottom, left, right coordinates.

Returns
-------
p: 3-tuple
   The point converted to simplex coordinates.
Convert coordinates for custom plots with limits

functions.name: ternary.helpers.get_conversion
documentation:
Get the conversion equations for each axis.

limits: dict of min and max values for the axes in the order blr.

functions.name: ternary.helpers.convert_coordinates_sequence
documentation:
Take a sequence of 3-tuples in data coordinates and convert them
to simplex coordinates for plotting. This is needed for custom
plots where the scale of the simplex axes is set within limits rather
than being defined by the scale parameter.

Parameters
----------
qs, sequence of 3-tuples
   The points to be plotted in data coordinates.

scale: int
    The scale parameter for the plot.
limits: dict
    keys = ['b','l','r']
    values = min,max data values for this axis.
axisorder: String giving the order of the axes for the coordinate tuple
    e.g. 'blr' for bottom, left, right coordinates.

Returns
-------
s, list of 3-tuples
   the points converted to simplex coordinates

functions.name: ternary.colormapping.get_cmap
documentation:
Loads a matplotlib colormap if specified or supplies the default.

Parameters
----------
cmap: string or matplotlib.colors.Colormap instance
    The name of the Matplotlib colormap to look up.

Returns
-------
The desired Matplotlib colormap

Raises
------
ValueError if colormap name is not recognized by Matplotlib
# Matplotlib Colormapping ##

functions.name: ternary.colormapping.colormapper
documentation:
Maps values to colors by normalizing within [a,b], obtaining rgba from the
given matplotlib color map for heatmap polygon coloring.

Parameters
----------
value: float
    The value to be colormapped
lower: float
    Lower bound of colors
upper: float
    Upper bound of colors
cmap: String or matplotlib.colors.Colormap (optional)
    Colormap object to prevent repeated lookup

Returns
-------
hex_, float
    The value mapped to an appropriate RGBA color value

functions.name: ternary.colormapping.colorbar_hack
documentation:
Colorbar hack to insert colorbar on ternary plot.

Called by heatmap, not intended for direct usage.

Parameters
----------
vmin: float
    Minimum value to portray in colorbar
vmax: float
    Maximum value to portray in colorbar
cmap: Matplotlib colormap
    Matplotlib colormap to use

functions.name: ternary.coordinates.represent_permutation
documentation:
Maps a permutation from the 'brl' specification to or from the '012'
internal representation.

functions.name: ternary.coordinates.compose_permutations
documentation:
Composes the two permutations p1 and p2 as if they were functions in the
symmetric group. That is, p3 = p1 o p2.

Parameters
----------
inner: string
    The inner (first applied) permutation, a string of 'brl'
outer: string, None
    The outer (second applied) permutation, a string of 'brl'

Returns
-------
composite, string
    The permutaton composite = outer o inner

functions.name: ternary.coordinates.coordinate_system
documentation: Returns the permutation corresponding to the coordinate system.

modules.name: ternary.plotting
documentation: Plotting functions: scatter, plot (curves), axis labelling.

functions.name: ternary.plotting.resize_drawing_canvas
documentation:
Makes sure the drawing surface is large enough to display projected
content.

Parameters
----------
ax: Matplotlib AxesSubplot, None
    The subplot to draw on.
scale: float, 1.0
    Simplex scale size.
## Drawing Helpers ###

functions.name: ternary.plotting.clear_matplotlib_ticks
documentation:
Clears the default matplotlib axes, or the one specified by the axis
argument.

Parameters
----------
ax: Matplotlib AxesSubplot, None
    The subplot to draw on.
axis: string, "both"
    The axis to clear: "x" or "horizontal", "y" or "vertical", or "both"

functions.name: ternary.plotting.plot
documentation:
Analogous to maplotlib.plot. Plots trajectory points where each point is a
tuple (x,y,z) satisfying x + y + z = scale (not checked). The tuples are
projected and plotted as a curve.

Parameters
----------
points: List of 3-tuples
    The list of tuples to be plotted as a connected curve.
ax: Matplotlib AxesSubplot, None
    The subplot to draw on.
kwargs:
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib.
# Curve Plotting ##

functions.name: ternary.plotting.plot_colored_trajectory
documentation:
Plots trajectories with changing color, simlar to `plot`. Trajectory points
are tuples (x,y,z) satisfying x + y + z = scale (not checked). The tuples are
projected and plotted as a curve.

Parameters
----------
points: List of 3-tuples
    The list of tuples to be plotted as a connected curve.
ax: Matplotlib AxesSubplot, None
    The subplot to draw on.
cmap: String or matplotlib.colors.Colormap, None
    The name of the Matplotlib colormap to use.
kwargs:
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib.

functions.name: ternary.plotting.scatter
documentation:
Plots trajectory points where each point satisfies x + y + z = scale.
First argument is a list or numpy array of tuples of length 3.

Parameters
----------
points: List of 3-tuples
    The list of tuples to be scatter-plotted.
ax: Matplotlib AxesSubplot, None
    The subplot to draw on.
colorbar: bool, False
    Show colorbar.
colormap: String or matplotlib.colors.Colormap, None
    The name of the Matplotlib colormap to use.
vmin: int, 0
    Minimum value for colorbar.
vmax: int, 1
    Maximum value for colorbar.
cb_kwargs: dict
    Any additional kwargs to pass to colorbar
kwargs:
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib.

modules.name: ternary.heatmapping
documentation: Various Heatmaps.

functions.name: ternary.heatmapping.blend_value
documentation:
Computes the average value of the three vertices of a triangle in the
simplex triangulation, where two of the vertices are on the lower
horizontal.
## Heatmap Triangulation Coordinates
# Triangular Heatmaps

functions.name: ternary.heatmapping.alt_blend_value
documentation:
Computes the average value of the three vertices of a triangle in the
simplex triangulation, where two of the vertices are on the upper
horizontal.

functions.name: ternary.heatmapping.triangle_coordinates
documentation:
Computes coordinates of the constituent triangles of a triangulation for the
simplex. These triangles are parallel to the lower axis on the lower side.

Parameters
----------
i,j,k: enumeration of the desired triangle

Returns
-------
A numpy array of coordinates of the hexagon (unprojected)

functions.name: ternary.heatmapping.alt_triangle_coordinates
documentation:
Computes coordinates of the constituent triangles of a triangulation for the
simplex. These triangles are parallel to the lower axis on the upper side.

Parameters
----------
i,j,k: enumeration of the desired triangle

Returns
-------
A numpy array of coordinates of the hexagon (unprojected)

functions.name: ternary.heatmapping.generate_hexagon_deltas
documentation:
Generates a dictionary of the necessary additive vectors to generate the
hexagon points for the hexagonal heatmap.
# Hexagonal Heatmaps ##

functions.name: ternary.heatmapping.hexagon_coordinates
documentation:
Computes coordinates of the constituent hexagons of a hexagonal heatmap.

Parameters
----------
i, j, k: enumeration of the desired hexagon

Returns
-------
A numpy array of coordinates of the hexagon (unprojected)

functions.name: ternary.heatmapping.polygon_generator
documentation:
Generator for the vertices of the polygon to be colored and its color,
depending on style. Called by heatmap.
# Heatmaps ##

functions.name: ternary.heatmapping.heatmap
documentation:
Plots heatmap of given color values.

Parameters
----------
data: dictionary
    A dictionary mapping the i, j polygon to the heatmap color, where
    i + j + k = scale.
scale: Integer
    The scale used to partition the simplex.
vmin: float, None
    The minimum color value, used to normalize colors. Computed if absent.
vmax: float, None
    The maximum color value, used to normalize colors. Computed if absent.
cmap: String or matplotlib.colors.Colormap, None
    The name of the Matplotlib colormap to use.
ax: Matplotlib AxesSubplot, None
    The subplot to draw on.
scientific: Bool, False
    Whether to use scientific notation for colorbar numbers.
style: String, "triangular"
    The style of the heatmap, "triangular", "dual-triangular" or "hexagonal"
colorbar: bool, True
    Show colorbar.
permutation: string, None
    A permutation of the coordinates
use_rgba: bool, False
    Use rgba color values
cbarlabel: string, None
    Text label for the colorbar
cb_kwargs: dict
    dict of kwargs to pass to colorbar

Returns
-------
ax: The matplotlib axis

functions.name: ternary.heatmapping.heatmapf
documentation:
Computes func on heatmap partition coordinates and plots heatmap. In other
words, computes the function on lattice points of the simplex (normalized
points) and creates a heatmap from the values.

Parameters
----------
func: Function
    A function of 3-tuples to be heatmapped
scale: Integer
    The scale used to partition the simplex
boundary: Bool, True
    Include the boundary points or not
cmap: String, None
    The name of the Matplotlib colormap to use
ax: Matplotlib axis object, None
    The axis to draw the colormap on
style: String, "triangular"
    The style of the heatmap, "triangular", "dual-triangular" or "hexagonal"
scientific: Bool, False
    Whether to use scientific notation for colorbar numbers.
colorbar: bool, True
    Show colorbar.
permutation: string, None
    A permutation of the coordinates
vmin: float
    The minimum color value, used to normalize colors.
vmax: float
    The maximum color value, used to normalize colors.
cb_kwargs: dict
    dict of kwargs to pass to colorbar

Returns
-------
ax, The matplotlib axis
# User Convenience Functions ##

functions.name: ternary.heatmapping.svg_polygon
documentation:
Create an svg triangle for the stationary heatmap.

Parameters
----------
coordinates: list
    The coordinates defining the polygon
color: string
    RGB color value e.g. #26ffd1

Returns
-------
string, the svg string for the polygon

functions.name: ternary.heatmapping.svg_heatmap
documentation:
Create a heatmap in SVG format. Intended for use with very large datasets,
which would require large amounts of RAM using matplotlib. You can convert
the image to another format with e.g. ImageMagick:

convert -density 1200 -resize -rotate 180 1000x1000 your.svg your.png

Parameters
----------

data: dictionary or k, v generator
    A dictionary mapping the i, j polygon to the heatmap color, where
    i + j + k = scale. If using a generator, style must be 'h'.
scale: Integer
    The scale used to partition the simplex.
filename: string
    The filename to write the SVG data to.
vmin: float
    The minimum color value, used to normalize colors.
vmax: float
    The maximum color value, used to normalize colors.
cmap: String or matplotlib.colors.Colormap, None
    The name of the Matplotlib colormap to use.
style: String, "h"
    The style of the heatmap, "triangular", "dual-triangular" or "hexagonal"
permutation: string, None
    A permutation of the coordinates

functions.name: ternary.heatmapping.background_color
documentation: Draws a triangle behind the plot to serve as the background color.

modules.name: ternary.lines
documentation: Line plotting functions, draw boundary and gridlines.

functions.name: ternary.lines.line
documentation:
Draws a line on `ax` from p1 to p2.

Parameters
----------
ax: Matplotlib AxesSubplot, None
    The subplot to draw on.
p1: 2-tuple
    The (x,y) starting coordinates
p2: 2-tuple
    The (x,y) ending coordinates
kwargs:
    Any kwargs to pass through to Matplotlib.
# Lines ##

functions.name: ternary.lines.horizontal_line
documentation:
Draws the i-th horizontal line parallel to the lower axis.

Parameters
----------
ax: Matplotlib AxesSubplot
    The subplot to draw on.
scale: float, 1.0
    Simplex scale size.
i: float
    The index of the line to draw
kwargs: Dictionary
    Any kwargs to pass through to Matplotlib.

functions.name: ternary.lines.left_parallel_line
documentation:
Draws the i-th line parallel to the left axis.

Parameters
----------
ax: Matplotlib AxesSubplot
    The subplot to draw on.
scale: float
    Simplex scale size.
i: float
    The index of the line to draw
kwargs: Dictionary
    Any kwargs to pass through to Matplotlib.

functions.name: ternary.lines.right_parallel_line
documentation:
Draws the i-th line parallel to the right axis.

Parameters
----------
ax: Matplotlib AxesSubplot
    The subplot to draw on.
scale: float
    Simplex scale size.
i: float
    The index of the line to draw
kwargs: Dictionary
    Any kwargs to pass through to Matplotlib.

functions.name: ternary.lines.boundary
documentation:
Plots the boundary of the simplex. Creates and returns matplotlib axis if
none given.

Parameters
----------
ax: Matplotlib AxesSubplot, None
    The subplot to draw on.
scale: float
    Simplex scale size.
kwargs:
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib.
axes_colors: dict
    Option for coloring boundaries different colors.
    e.g. {'l': 'g'} for coloring the left axis boundary green
# Boundary, Gridlines ##

functions.name: ternary.lines.merge_dicts
documentation:
Given two dicts, merge them into a new dict as a shallow copy.

Parameters
----------
base: dict
    The base dictionary.
updates: dict
    Secondary dictionary whose values override the base.

functions.name: ternary.lines.gridlines
documentation:
Plots grid lines excluding boundary.

Parameters
----------
ax: Matplotlib AxesSubplot, None
    The subplot to draw on.
scale: float
    Simplex scale size.
multiple: float, None
    Specifies which inner gridelines to draw. For example, if scale=30 and
    multiple=6, only 5 inner gridlines will be drawn.
horizontal_kwargs: dict, None
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib for horizontal gridlines
left_kwargs: dict, None
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib for left parallel gridlines
right_kwargs: dict, None
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib for right parallel gridlines
kwargs:
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib, if not using
    horizontal_kwargs, left_kwargs, or right_kwargs

functions.name: ternary.lines.ticks
documentation:
Sets tick marks and labels.

Parameters
----------
ax: Matplotlib AxesSubplot, None
    The subplot to draw on.
scale: float, 1.0
    Simplex scale size.
ticks: list of strings, None
    The tick labels
locations: list of points, None
    The locations of the ticks
multiple: float, None
    Specifies which ticks gridelines to draw. For example, if scale=30 and
    multiple=6, only 5 ticks will be drawn.
axis: str, 'b'
    The axis or axes to draw the ticks for. `axis` must be a substring of
    'lrb' (as sets)
offset: float, 0.01
    controls the length of the ticks
clockwise: bool, False
    Draw ticks marks clockwise or counterclockwise
axes_colors: Dict, None
    Option to color ticks differently for each axis, 'l', 'r', 'b'
    e.g. {'l': 'g', 'r':'b', 'b': 'y'}
tick_formats: None, Dict, Str
    If None, all axes will be labelled with ints. If Dict, the keys are
    'b', 'l' and 'r' and the values are format strings e.g. "%.3f" for
    a float with 3 decimal places or "%.3e" for scientific format with
    3 decimal places or "%d" for ints. If tick_formats is a string, it
    is assumed that this is a format string to be applied to all axes.
kwargs:
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib.

modules.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot
documentation: Wrapper class for all ternary plotting functions.

functions.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.figure
documentation:
Wraps a Matplotlib AxesSubplot or generates a new one. Emulates matplotlib's
> figure, ax = plt.subplots()

Parameters
----------
ax: AxesSubplot, None
    The matplotlib AxesSubplot to wrap
scale: float, None
    The scale factor of the ternary plot

functions.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.mpl_redraw_callback
documentation:
Callback to properly rotate and redraw text labels when the plot is drawn
or resized.

Parameters
----------
event: a matplotlib event
    either 'resize_event' or 'draw_event'
tax: TernaryAxesSubplot
     the TernaryAxesSubplot

classes.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot._connect_callbacks
documentation: Connect resize matplotlib callbacks.

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.get_axes
documentation: Returns the underlying matplotlib AxesSubplot object.

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.get_figure
documentation: Return the underlying matplotlib figure object.

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.set_axis_limits
documentation:
Set min and max data limits for each of the three axes.

axis_limits = dict
    keys are 'b','l' and 'r' for the three axes
    vals are lists of the min and max values for the axis in
    data units.

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.set_title
documentation: Sets the title on the underlying matplotlib AxesSubplot.
Title and Axis Labels

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.left_axis_label
documentation:
Sets the label on the left axis.

Parameters
----------
label: String
    The axis label
position: 3-Tuple of floats, None
    The position of the text label
rotation: float, 60
    The angle of rotation of the label
offset: float,
    Used to compute the distance of the label from the axis
kwargs:
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib.

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.right_axis_label
documentation:
Sets the label on the right axis.

Parameters
----------
label: String
    The axis label
position: 3-Tuple of floats, None
    The position of the text label
rotation: float, -60
    The angle of rotation of the label
offset: float,
    Used to compute the distance of the label from the axis
kwargs:
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib.

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.bottom_axis_label
documentation:
Sets the label on the bottom axis.

Parameters
----------
label: String
    The axis label
position: 3-Tuple of floats, None
    The position of the text label
rotation: float, 0
    The angle of rotation of the label
offset: float,
    Used to compute the distance of the label from the axis
kwargs:
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib.

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.right_corner_label
documentation:
Sets the label on the right corner (complements left axis).

Parameters
----------
label: String
    The axis label
position: 3-Tuple of floats, None
    The position of the text label
rotation: float, 0
    The angle of rotation of the label
offset: float,
    Used to compute the distance of the label from the axis
kwargs:
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib.

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.left_corner_label
documentation:
Sets the label on the left corner (complements right axis.)

Parameters
----------
label: string
    The axis label
position: 3-Tuple of floats, None
    The position of the text label
rotation: float, 0
    The angle of rotation of the label
offset: float,
    Used to compute the distance of the label from the axis
kwargs:
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib.

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.top_corner_label
documentation:
Sets the label on the bottom axis.

Parameters
----------
label: String
    The axis label
position: 3-Tuple of floats, None
    The position of the text label
rotation: float, 0
    The angle of rotation of the label
offset: float,
    Used to compute the distance of the label from the axis
kwargs:
    Any kwargs to pass through to matplotlib.

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.boundary
documentation: Boundary and Gridlines

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.line
documentation: Various Lines

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.close
documentation: Matplotlib passthroughs

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.clear_matplotlib_ticks
documentation: Clears the default matplotlib ticks.
Axis ticks

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.get_ticks_from_axis_limits
documentation:
Taking self._axis_limits and self._boundary_scale get the scaled
ticks for all three axes and store them in self._ticks under the
keys 'b' for bottom, 'l' for left and 'r' for right axes.

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.set_custom_ticks
documentation:
Having called get_ticks_from_axis_limits, set the custom ticks on the
plot.

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.resize_drawing_canvas
documentation: Redrawing and resizing

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot._redraw_labels
documentation: Redraw axis labels, typically after draw or resize events.

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.convert_coordinates
documentation:
Convert data coordinates to simplex coordinates for plotting
in the case that axis limits have been applied.

methods.name: ternary.ternary_axes_subplot.TernaryAxesSubplot.scatter
documentation: Various Plots

Wrapper for python-ternary and matplotlib figure. Parameters for member
functions simply pass through to ternary's functions with the same names.
This class manages the matplotlib axes, the scale, and the boundary scale
to ease the use of ternary plotting functions.
